author: doll-agent
content: Audit. The ToArrow/ToArrowBool and FromArrow/FromArrowBool split is intentional and reflects Arrow's underlying architecture: BooleanArray is bit-packed and not a PrimitiveArray, so Rust's coherence rules force the bool variant into a sibling trait. The trait names already document this (ToArrowBool/FromArrowBool); the alternative — a unified trait with associated output types — would still require special-casing bool and would obscure rather than improve the situation. Closing as accepted-as-status-quo.
